codeReview.summary: Feature implementation successfully replaces Navbar with a collapsible Sidebar component. All required navigation sections are present (Dashboard, Master Data with COA/Aspek Kerja/Blok sub-items, Kas/Bank/Gudang modules, Admin-only Sync and User Management). The sidebar supports vertical scrolling and collapse functionality. TypeScript compilation and linting pass successfully.

issues.description: Toggle button uses hardcoded `top: 16` (4rem) positioning with `position: absolute`. This may not align correctly if sidebar header height changes. Consider using a relative positioning approach or CSS variable for the header height.

Custom scrollbar classes `scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-primary-500 scrollbar-track-primary-800` require tailwind-scrollbar plugin or equivalent custom CSS. Verify these classes are properly configured in tailwind.config.js.

The sidebar uses `flex-1` in the nav container but `height: 100%` on the aside element. This could cause issues in certain browser layouts. Consider using `min-h-screen` or explicit height constraints on parent containers for more robust layout.

The `isCollapsed` prop controls sidebar width but the toggle button is positioned `absolute` outside the sidebar. When collapsed to w-16, the toggle button at `-right-3` may overlap content next to the sidebar. Consider z-index management or repositioning.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified this as a frontend task and invoked the frontend-worker skill instead of the generic worker skill. Transcript shows: 'The "worker" skill doesn\'t exist, but I see "frontend-worker" which is appropriate since this is a UI sidebar navigation feature.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used npm commands (npm run dev, npm run build, npm run lint, npm run typecheck) that match the services.yaml definitions. No undocumented services were started.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows npm run dev, npm run build, npm run lint, npm run typecheck commands were executed successfully

summary: PASS - Feature implementation is complete and correct. The Sidebar component successfully replaces the Navbar with all required functionality: left-side placement, expandable Master Data sub-menu (COA, Aspek Kerja, Blok), vertical scrolling support, collapsible toggle, and admin-only Sync/User Management sections. Navigation logic in App.tsx was preserved. TypeScript and lint checks pass. Four minor non-blocking issues identified related to CSS positioning and scrollbar configuration, but these do not prevent the feature from functioning correctly.
